title: PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB
description: Returns the count of subscribers of shard channels.
complexity: O(N) for the SHARDNUMSUB subcommand, where N is the number of requested shard channels

params.description: Shard channel names to get subscriber counts for
params.name: *args

returns.description: Map of shard channel names to subscriber counts

params.description: Shard channel names to get subscriber counts for
params.name: channels

returns.description: Map of shard channel names to subscriber counts

params.description: Shard channel names to get subscriber counts for
params.name: channels

returns.description: Map of shard channel names to subscriber counts

params.description: Shard channel names to get subscriber counts for
params.name: $argument

returns.description: Map of shard channel names to subscriber counts

content:
Returns the number of subscribers for the specified shard channels. Note that it is valid to call this command without channels, in this case it will just return an empty list. Cluster note: in a Redis Cluster, [`PUBSUB`]()'s replies in a cluster only report information from the node's Pub/Sub context, rather than the entire cluster. ## Examples ``` > PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB orders 1) "orders" 2) (integer) 1 ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="pubsub-shardnumsub-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the number of subscribers per shard channel, each even element (including the 0th) is channel name, each odd element is the number of subscribers. -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the number of subscribers per shard channel, each even element (including the 0th) is channel name, each odd element is the number of subscribers. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Returns the number of subscribers for the specified shard channels.

Note that it is valid to call this command without channels, in this case it will just return an empty list.

Cluster note: in a Redis Cluster, [`PUBSUB`]()'s replies in a cluster only report information from the node's Pub/Sub context, rather than the entire cluster.

sections.content:
Returns the number of subscribers for the specified shard channels.

Note that it is valid to call this command without channels, in this case it will just return an empty list.

Cluster note: in a Redis Cluster, [`PUBSUB`]()'s replies in a cluster only report information from the node's Pub/Sub context, rather than the entire cluster.
